content:
A programming language that speaks fluent dog.

Dogesh is an interpreted programming language written in Python, where every
keyword is inspired by the language of our four-legged friends. Instead of
writing print, you write WOOF. Instead of input, you BARK. Variables are
remembered with SNIFF, loops become CHASE, and functions are just clever
TRICKs.

It started as a joke. Like many questionable ideas in software engineering, it
quickly became a serious project.

Features

‚ÄĘ ūüźĺ Human-readable dog syntax

‚ÄĘ ūüźĺ Written entirely in Python

‚ÄĘ ūüźĺ Modular architecture

‚ÄĘ ūüźĺ Easy to extend with new language features

‚ÄĘ ūüźĺ Educational compiler/interpreter project

Example

WOOF "Hello, Human!"

Output

Hello, Human!

Variables

SNIFF name = "Raj"
WOOF name

Loop

CHASE 3
WOOF "Woof!"
TAIL

Conditional

GRRR age > 5
WOOF "Big dog"
SNARL
WOOF "Puppy"
TAIL

Planned Keywords

Dogesh
Meaning

WOOF
Print output

BARK
Ask for user input

FETCH
Retrieve the latest input

SNIFF
Declare a variable

GRRR
If statement

SNARL
Else

TAIL
End a block

CHASE
Loop

CHASEWHILE
While loop

TRICK
Function definition

ROLL
Function call

TREAT
Return a value

NAP
Exit program

ZOOMIES()
Random number

TAILSIZE()
Length of a string

MAILMAN()
Current timestamp

Roadmap

Version 0.1

‚ÄĘ Project structure

‚ÄĘ Token definitions

‚ÄĘ Lexer

‚ÄĘ Parser

‚ÄĘ AST

‚ÄĘ Interpreter

‚ÄĘ WOOF

Version 0.2

‚ÄĘ Variables (SNIFF)

‚ÄĘ Strings

‚ÄĘ Numbers

‚ÄĘ Arithmetic

Version 0.3

‚ÄĘ Input (BARK)

‚ÄĘ FETCH

‚ÄĘ Expressions

‚ÄĘ Comparisons

Version 0.4

‚ÄĘ GRRR

‚ÄĘ SNARL

‚ÄĘ TAIL

Version 0.5

‚ÄĘ CHASE

‚ÄĘ CHASEWHILE

Version 0.6

‚ÄĘ Functions

‚ÄĘ Recursion

‚ÄĘ Local scope

Version 1.0

‚ÄĘ Standard Library

‚ÄĘ Modules

‚ÄĘ Error reporting

‚ÄĘ REPL

‚ÄĘ Package manager (maybe)

‚ÄĘ Bytecode virtual machine (future)

Goals

Dogesh is designed as:

‚ÄĘ A fun programming language

‚ÄĘ An educational compiler/interpreter project

‚ÄĘ A playground for experimenting with language design

‚ÄĘ A clean, modular Python codebase that demonstrates how programming languages work internally

Why?

Because every programming language started with someone asking:

"What if..."

This one simply asked:

"What if computers understood dogs?"

License

Made with ‚ėē, ūüź∂, and an unreasonable amount of barking.
